title: Why did Gendos glasses break
wikitext:
[]: Why did Gendo's glasses break?             Quote Post by xelloss_ssj » Mon Sep 09, 2013 5:08 am Hi everybody, I'm new to the forum, I hope this is not the 1000th topic opened asking this question: I did read a lot around here and searched for this specific query and found nothing. Please don't bash me too hard if I missed it. So, back to the original question: why did Gendo's glasses break when he open the entry plug in order to extract Rei? They do not break immediately after the fall, but rather after coming in contact with the LCL. Is there any theory why this should happen? I'm really puzzled by this because the scene seems very deliberate. Thanks to anyone who will manage to shed some light, xel

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Sep 09, 2013 5:31 am Heat. It started melting.

[]:             Quote Post by xelloss_ssj » Mon Sep 09, 2013 5:38 am Thought so too at first, and heat by the exteral of the entry plug should be certainly sufficient to fuse the plastic part, but what about the lenses? It seems a bit too extreme, doesn't it?

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Sep 09, 2013 7:01 am Thermal expansion. The glass expanded fast and irregularly, and the frames expanded due to the heat. Both bent out of shape and the glass cracked.

[]:             Quote Post by tootiredtoriot » Mon Sep 09, 2013 7:15 pm mm, if the LCL was hot enough to break glass surely Rei would be in worse shape / cooked though? or should we just say it's visually interesting and just enjoy it without looking further into it.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Sep 09, 2013 7:19 pm They're broken because they represent his point of view, which is the only one that matters to Rei, which is why she in turn keeps them as a keepsake. Later, Shinji looks through them and sees Rei as a blur because Gendo no longer sees her as a person. I want to say the change in his glasses from his old clear spectacles to his standard orange reflective ones means something but I'm having a hard time saying why the change would occur in that particular moment, given that his apparent concern for Rei is weirdly out of character and inconsistent with the rest of his characterization on the show. It may be suggesting that the attempts to retrieve Yui from the Eva skewed his perspective, with Rei acting as a stand-in for Yui; the glasses crack when they're sitting in LCL.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Sep 09, 2013 7:27 pm Chuckman, that's a completely different kind of "why" from what the OP was asking. It's not exactly a complaint, but, it is so far out of left field of the spirit of this topic that it probably deserves a split in the near future, discussing the thematic meaning of those glasses.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Sep 09, 2013 7:29 pm It just occurred to me that constantly wearing orange-tinted glasses would be like looking at the world through LCL at all times.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Sep 10, 2013 1:25 am tootiredtoriot wrote: mm, if the LCL was hot enough to break glass surely Rei would be in worse shape / cooked though?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Sep 10, 2013 2:59 am Physics and Eva, Tines... Can't you hear the catgirls' screams from here?

[]:             Quote Post by xelloss_ssj » Tue Sep 10, 2013 3:41 am Monk Ed wrote: Chuckman, that's a completely different kind of "why" from what the OP was asking. It's not exactly a complaint, but, it is so far out of left field of the spirit of this topic that it probably deserves a split in the near future, discussing the thematic meaning of those glasses.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Sep 10, 2013 7:43 am Well, old-fashioned teacups have been known to crack when hot tea is suddenly poured into a cold cup, thanks to irregular expansion. Gendo got his glasses post-2I and it's possible that modern glass formulae had been replaced by archaic ones, hence the vulnerability to temperature. One hell of a long shot, though. The alternative is the simpler explanation - Anno didn't know that glass didn't usually expand that much from heat, and goofed up.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Sep 11, 2013 9:55 am Plastic is one of the things that melt fairly easily, which is why you don't microwave it. Gendo's glasses had plastic rims. (rather cheap for someone who, according to Ep21, swims in money, but that's just how he is) The heat from the LCL slightly melted the plastic out of shape, only slightly enough to put enough strain on the glasses to crack them. Then again, breaking glass itself through sudden temperature changes IS a thing. Those glasses were, in any case, ridiculously cheap looking. I doubt it was any sort of quality glass. And look what happens when they break - instead of getting new ones, he starts using some sunglasses (with appropiate lenses) he had lying around somewhere. Then again, another explanation for the sunglasses could be that he's spent so much time underground, he can't even STAND the sunlight anymore. He's so accustumed to darkness that he keeps them on when NERV has a freakin' blackout. Throughout the entire series, we only see his present-day incarnation at the surface twice , if you count that ship in antarctica. Actually, this is exactly the kind of insight I was looking for: while temperature may be enough to break the glasses, it would have been easier to make them shatter upon falling.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Sep 11, 2013 3:44 pm Not all plastic is easy to melt. My plastic frames for example, you have to heat with a torch in order to re-bend its shape (because you have to get it hot enough to make the metal core malleable). Plenty of plastics are microwave safe, e.g. everything you get in take-out containers. All of my plastic dishware and cups are microwave safe and even fine to put in an oven. But those are types of plastics that are made to endure heat. It's obviously not like a plastic toy that you leave on the dashboard of your car and it melts from the heat of the sun. I don't know of any glasses of any kind that are made of glass. I did work for a lens company when I was in highschool (in the late 80's) and was told they stopped using glass decades ago, and everything, except for some rare extreme cases (like special safety glasses or bizarre prescriptions) is now plastic. And the technology has just kept getting better. It could be that Gendo had some special dual purpose glasses that were also safety glasses. Not safety glasses in the sense that large metal objects could fly out of table saws, but corrosive materials that could react with plastic but not glass. Or something like that.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Sep 12, 2013 7:39 am Plastic lenses can crack too, if not that easily. No shards fliying everwhere is certainly a plus. And ppl, remember that we actually DO see the framing of these glasses slightly lose its shape just before it cracked? It melted.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Sep 12, 2013 9:42 am The frames clearly deform before the lenses crack.

[]:             Quote Post by Falcon_of_the_Sun » Sun Sep 15, 2013 12:48 pm Could it be that the LCL changes in temperature (getting either colder than it was in the entry plug or hotter because the floor was heated up by the friction/contact with the rocketing entry plug) which in turn causes a change in its density that bends the frame and cracks the lense? Something along the lines of winter black ice cracking and ruining the tarmac of a road? I'd be inclined to believe it's some sort of goof though. Anno wanted the show the glasses actually breaking but that couldn't happen in the midst of the action. Hence the slow cracking once the climax is past and Rei has been rescued.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sun Sep 15, 2013 2:40 pm It's drawn out of the action and given full frame focus and significant because it Means Something, it's just a bit obscure in its meaning than the common imagery, like hands and feet.
